subject:
Re: Phase plate subtomo averaging: M always crashes at CTF
 refinement?
body:
Hi all,

Late to the party but a little clarity - this issue is not VPP specific, it 
happens when you refine multiple species and one tilt-series has no 
particles for one of the species. 

Good luck!

Alister

On Friday, 16 December 2022 at 09:45:02 UTC joe dobbs wrote:

> Hi Pranav,
>
> Thanks, yeah, it's multi-species refinement. Thanks for confirming- at 
> least it's a consistent issue!
>
> best,
> joe
> On Friday, December 16, 2022 at 12:46:57 AM UTC+1 Pranav Shah wrote:
>
>> Hi,
>> Are you trying to perform CTF refinement on multiple species? I have
>> come across this error recently and there is no straight way out of
>> it. If you can, then try to refine each species singly. Also try
>> 1.1.0....
>> Best,
>> Pranav
>> --
>> Pranav Shah
>> Postdoctoral Research Fellow.
>>
>> Division of Structural Biology,
>> Wellcome Trust Centre for Human Genetics,
>> University of Oxford,
>> Roosevelt Drive, Oxford OX3 7BN,
>> UK
>>
>> On Thu, Dec 15, 2022 at 11:23 PM joe dobbs <dobbsj...@gmail.com> wrote:
>> >
>> > Hello,
>> >
>> > When trying to refine any CTF parameter on VPP subtomos M always 
>> crashes. Does anyone also have this problem and/or know a cause? It's some 
>> sort of key error, it seems.
>> >
>> > Many thanks,
>> > joe
>> >
>> > Error message:
>> > Running optimization iteration 1/3, defocus grid search
>> > Running optimization iteration 1/3, CTF parameters
>> > System.Collections.Generic.KeyNotFoundException: The given key was not 
>> present in the dictionary.
>> > at System.ThrowHelper.ThrowKeyNotFoundException()
>> > at System.Collections.Generic.Dictionary`2.get_Item(TKey key)
>> > at 
>> Warp.TiltSeries.<>c__DisplayClass87_2.<PerformMultiParticleRefinement>b__29(Double[] 
>> input) in D:\Dev\warp2\WarpLib\TiltSeries.cs:line 4511
>> > at 
>> Accord.Math.Optimization.NonlinearObjectiveFunction.CheckGradient(Func`2 
>> value, Double[] probe)
>> > at Accord.Math.Optimization.BaseGradientOptimizationMethod.Maximize()
>> > at Warp.TiltSeries.PerformMultiParticleRefinement(String 
>> workingDirectory, ProcessingOptionsMPARefine optionsMPA, Species[] 
>> allSpecies, DataSource dataSource, Image gainRef, DefectModel defectMap, 
>> Action`1 progressCallback) in D:\Dev\warp2\WarpLib\TiltSeries.cs:line 5101
>> > at WarpWorker.Program.Main(String[] args) in 
>> D:\Dev\warp2\WarpWorker\Program.cs:line 294
>> >
>> > --
>> > You received this message because you are subscribed to the Google 
>> Groups "Warp" group.
